meaning: To reveal a secret
usage: Don't spill the beans about the surprise party!

meaning: To endure a painful or difficult situation
usage: I hate going to the dentist, but I'll just have to bite the bullet.

meaning: Something that is very easy to do
usage: That exam was a piece of cake; I finished it in twenty minutes.

meaning: Feeling slightly unwell or sick
usage: I’m feeling a bit under the weather, so I’ll stay home today.

meaning: To start a conversation in a social setting
usage: He told a joke to break the ice at the start of the meeting.

meaning: To stop working on something
usage: We've been at this for ten hours; let's call it a day.

meaning: To skip unnecessary details and reach the main point
usage: I don't have much time, so please just cut to the chase.

phrase: Hit the nail on the head
meaning: To describe exactly what is causing a situation
usage: You hit the nail on the head when you said the project lacked focus.

meaning: Something that happens very rarely
usage: I only see my old high school friends once in a blue moon.

meaning: A major problem that everyone is avoiding
usage: We need to talk about the budget; it's the elephant in the room.

meaning: To work late into the night
usage: She’s been burning the midnight oil to finish her thesis.

meaning: To feel nervous before a big event
usage: He got cold feet just before he was supposed to go on stage.

meaning: A good thing that seemed bad at first
usage: Losing that job was a blessing in disguise because I found a better one.

meaning: Starting over because a previous attempt failed
usage: The prototype didn't work, so it's back to the drawing board.

meaning: Avoiding the main topic
usage: Stop beating around the bush and tell me what you really think.

meaning: It's better to arrive late than not at all
usage: I finally finished the report; better late than never!

phrase: Bite off more than you can chew
meaning: Taking on a task that is too big
usage: I think I bit off more than I could chew by taking three extra shifts.

phrase: By the skin of your teeth
meaning: Narrowly escaping a disaster
usage: I passed the test by the skin of my teeth.

meaning: To become difficult to control
usage: The party got out of hand when fifty uninvited people showed up.

phrase: Give the benefit of the doubt
meaning: To believe someone without proof
usage: He’s late again, but let's give him the benefit of the doubt.

meaning: To do more than is required
usage: The hotel staff really went the extra mile to make us comfortable.

meaning: To remain persistent during a difficult time
usage: I know the training is tough, but hang in there!

meaning: Not knowing something makes you happier
usage: I didn't know the house was haunted, and ignorance was bliss.

meaning: To summarize briefly
usage: In a nutshell, we need more funding to continue the project.

meaning: To stay positive in a tough situation
usage: Keep your chin up; things will get better soon.

meaning: To be too late for an opportunity
usage: I waited too long to buy tickets and I missed the boat.

meaning: You must work hard to achieve results
usage: I’ve been at the gym for two hours; no pain, no gain.

meaning: Being alert and efficient
usage: The new assistant is really on the ball with his scheduling.

meaning: To calm down and act normally
usage: You need to pull yourself together before the interview starts.

meaning: Everything is going well up to this point
usage: We’ve finished the first half of the project; so far, so good.

meaning: When the person you are talking about appears
usage: Did you see what Mark did? Oh, speak of the devil, here he is.

meaning: An ideal situation with two different benefits
usage: Working from home gives her the best of both worlds.

meaning: To stay together regardless of circumstances
usage: They have been best friends through thick and thin.

meaning: To understand something complicated
usage: I’m trying to wrap my head around this new software.

phrase: Your guess is as good as mine
meaning: To have no idea about something
usage: Why did the power go out? Your guess is as good as mine.

meaning: Something very common and easy to find
usage: Travel blogs are a dime a dozen these days.

meaning: To make a bad situation even worse
usage: They fired him, and to add insult to injury, they kept his bonus.

meaning: Following the wrong line of thought
usage: If you think I stole your pen, you're barking up the wrong tree.

meaning: A way to wish someone good luck
usage: I know you'll be great in the play tonight; break a leg!

meaning: To destroy relationships or paths back
usage: Don't burn your bridges by being rude when you quit your job.

phrase: Cost an arm and a leg
meaning: To be extremely expensive
usage: The new luxury car cost him an arm and a leg.

phrase: Every cloud has a silver lining
meaning: Every bad situation has some good aspect
usage: I missed the train, but met an old friend; every cloud has a silver lining.

phrase: Get a taste of your own medicine
meaning: To be treated as you have treated others
usage: The bully finally got a taste of his own medicine today.

meaning: To prevent something from approaching or hurting you
usage: She used vitamins to keep the cold at bay.

meaning: To try every possible way to find something
usage: We will leave no stone unturned to find the lost keys.

meaning: To take credit for someone else's work
usage: I was about to announce the news when she stole my thunder.

phrase: Take it with a grain of salt
meaning: To not take something too seriously

meaning: Uncertain or not yet decided

meaning: What people do is more important than what they say
usage: He says he loves me, but actions speak louder than words.

meaning: To make a bad situation even worse
usage: His angry response only added fuel to the fire.

meaning: Fully listening and paying attention

meaning: To release stress or strong emotion

meaning: To be the person who makes the decisions

meaning: A change in one's opinion or feelings

meaning: No matter what the circumstances are

meaning: Being too inquisitive can lead to trouble
usage: Don't look in that drawer; curiosity killed the cat!

meaning: Practical, realistic, and humble
usage: Even though he's famous, he's very down to earth.

meaning: To irritate or annoy someone very much
usage: That loud music is driving me up the wall!

meaning: In very good physical health
usage: My grandfather is 80, but he's still fit as a fiddle.

meaning: To start a process or project
usage: Let's have a meeting to get the ball rolling on the new design.

meaning: To try something

meaning: To fail spectacularly
usage: The new business went down in flames within six months.

meaning: To begin studying hard

meaning: To go to bed or go to sleep

meaning: To be in trouble

meaning: At the last possible moment

meaning: It's not that difficult to understand

meaning: To join a popular trend

meaning: To hope for good luck
usage: I’m keeping my fingers crossed for your interview.

meaning: The final problem that makes a situation intolerable

meaning: To accidentally reveal a secret

meaning: Think carefully before you act

meaning: To lose an ability you used to have

meaning: Happening unexpectedly

meaning: Extremely happy or excited
usage: She’s over the moon about her new promotion.

meaning: To decide what to do as the situation develops

meaning: To spoil someone's plans or mood

meaning: To find a hidden meaning

meaning: A general, practical rule

meaning: To agree with someone completely

meaning: To clarify or explain something
usage: Can you shed some light on how this feature works?

meaning: To be undecided between two choices

meaning: Light conversation about unimportant things

meaning: Everything; all the way

meaning: To think creatively and unconventionally

meaning: To give up or admit defeat

meaning: Time passes very quickly

meaning: Meeting the required standard

phrase: Wait for the dust to settle

meaning: A search that is completely unsuccessful or pointless

meaning: You aren't interested in what you're doing

meaning: To be quiet and stop talking

meaning: Asking someone what they are thinking

meaning: In the same difficult situation as others

meaning: Someone who is cherished above all others

meaning: To be perfectly healthy or correct

meaning: At the very last moment

meaning: In a very difficult or desperate situation

phrase: Barking is worse than the bite

meaning: Wasting time on something already settled

meaning: To try extremely hard to please someone

meaning: To work too hard without getting enough rest

meaning: To stop doing something or end a relationship

meaning: Ensure your information is accurate

meaning: Holding a grudge or being easily angered

meaning: To tell the truth about something secret

meaning: Be grateful for the good things in your life

meaning: Defeated or completely exhausted

meaning: To do the same thing as someone before you

meaning: Other people's lives always seem better than yours

meaning: Without a doubt; easily
